project.name: ReasonGraph MVP Examples
project.description: Seed examples for testing business reasoning and disagreement structuring.

issue.title: Pricing strategy
issue.business_context: The company is considering whether to increase prices next quarter in response to margin pressure, rising costs, and revenue targets.
issue.decision_question: Should the company increase prices by 15%, keep prices unchanged, or use a segmented pricing strategy?
issue.in_scope: Gross margin, churn risk, customer segments, competitor pricing, revenue targets, willingness to pay, implementation complexity.
issue.out_of_scope: General macroeconomic debates, unrelated brand strategy, long-term product roadmap.

positions.label: Increase prices by 15%
positions.recommendation: Apply a 15% price increase next quarter to protect gross margin.
positions.reasoning_text: Costs have increased and current prices no longer support the target gross margin. If the company delays the increase, it may need to cut spending or accept lower profitability.
positions.claims_text: Gross margin is below target. Cost inflation has not been fully passed to customers. A price increase would improve unit economics.
positions.assumptions_text: Most customers will tolerate a 15% increase. Competitors are also facing cost pressure. The company can communicate the increase without damaging trust.
positions.evidence_text: Recent margin reports show declining gross margin. Supplier costs have increased over the last two quarters.
positions.criteria_text: Gross margin, revenue protection, implementation speed, financial predictability.
positions.risks_text: Customer churn, negative customer reaction, sales team resistance.
positions.would_change_mind: This position would weaken if churn modeling shows that the price increase would reduce net revenue.

positions.label: Keep prices unchanged
positions.recommendation: Avoid a general price increase because churn risk is too high.
positions.reasoning_text: Customers are price-sensitive and several accounts are already considering alternatives. A broad price increase could trigger churn and damage commercial relationships.
positions.claims_text: A 15% price increase would increase churn risk. Some competitors may keep prices stable to win accounts. Sales teams will face stronger objections.
positions.assumptions_text: Customers have viable alternatives. Price is a major driver of retention. Account managers can defend current pricing better than a price increase.
positions.evidence_text: Sales feedback indicates higher price sensitivity. Some recent renewal conversations included budget objections.
positions.criteria_text: Customer retention, net revenue, relationship stability, competitive positioning.
positions.risks_text: Margin pressure remains unresolved. The company may underprice its value. Finance targets may be missed.
positions.would_change_mind: This position would weaken if customer segmentation shows that high-value segments can tolerate higher prices.

positions.label: Use segmented pricing
positions.recommendation: Avoid a blanket increase and apply segmented pricing based on customer value, usage, and willingness to pay.
positions.reasoning_text: A segmented approach can improve margins while reducing churn risk. Customers with higher usage or stronger value realization may tolerate increases better than price-sensitive segments.
positions.claims_text: Customer segments differ in willingness to pay. A blanket price increase is less precise than segmented pricing. Segmented pricing can balance margin and retention.
positions.assumptions_text: The company has enough customer data to segment pricing. Sales and billing systems can support differentiated pricing. Customers will accept segment-based changes if communicated clearly.
positions.evidence_text: Usage data, renewal history, and account size can be used to identify segments.
positions.criteria_text: Margin improvement, churn control, fairness, implementation complexity, customer value alignment.
positions.risks_text: Operational complexity, perceived unfairness, implementation delays.
positions.would_change_mind: This position would weaken if the company cannot operationalize segmentation quickly or fairly.
